## 10 Austral…          1 2013-01-16  13.4         1.06     94.2 1473.        -2.05
## # ℹ 2,621 more rows
## # ℹ 20 more variables: VIX_lag1 <dbl>, VIX_lag2 <dbl>, VIX_lag3 <dbl>,
## #   VIX_lag4 <dbl>, VIX_lag5 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag1 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag2 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOil_lag3 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag4 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag5 <dbl>,
## #   SP500_lag1 <dbl>, SP500_lag2 <dbl>, SP500_lag3 <dbl>, SP500_lag4 <dbl>,
## #   SP500_lag5 <dbl>, Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag1 <dbl>,
## #   Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag2 <dbl>, Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag3 <dbl>, …
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ExchangeRate = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIX_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ExchangeRate"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffGDP ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ExchangeRate - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)], 
                     data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIX, ExchangeRate, CrudeOil, SP500, RelDiffGDP) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIX_lag1 = lag(VIX, 1),
      VIX_lag2 = lag(VIX, 2),
      VIX_lag3 = lag(VIX, 3),
      VIX_lag4 = lag(VIX, 4),
      VIX_lag5 = lag(VIX, 5),
      CrudeOil_lag1 = lag(CrudeOil, 1),
      CrudeOil_lag2 = lag(CrudeOil, 2),
      CrudeOil_lag3 = lag(CrudeOil, 3),
      CrudeOil_lag4 = lag(CrudeOil, 4),
      CrudeOil_lag5 = lag(CrudeOil, 5),
      SP500_lag1 = lag(SP500, 1),
      SP500_lag2 = lag(SP500, 2),
      SP500_lag3 = lag(SP500, 3),
      SP500_lag4 = lag(SP500, 4),
      SP500_lag5 = lag(SP500, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ExchangeRate) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ExchangeRate), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ExchangeRate, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ExchangeRate = !!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ExchangeRate))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ExchangeRate, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ExchangeRate, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "Exchange Rate",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ExchangeRate_Lag3", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ExchangeRate_3", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

## # A tibble: 2,631 × 28
##    Country   Country_ID Date         VIX ExchangeRate CrudeOil SP500 RelDiffGDP
##    <chr>          <dbl> <date>     <dbl>        <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 Australia          1 2013-01-03  14.6         1.05     92.9 1459.    0.00151
##  2 Australia          1 2013-01-04  13.8         1.05     93.1 1466.    0.00151
##  3 Australia          1 2013-01-07  13.8         1.05     93.2 1462.    0.00151
##  4 Australia          1 2013-01-08  13.6         1.05     93.2 1457.    0.00151
##  5 Australia          1 2013-01-09  13.8         1.05     93.1 1461.    0.00151
##  6 Australia          1 2013-01-10  13.5         1.06     93.8 1472.    0.00151
##  7 Australia          1 2013-01-11  13.4         1.05     93.6 1472.    0.00151
##  8 Australia          1 2013-01-14  13.5         1.06     94.1 1471.    0.00151
##  9 Australia          1 2013-01-15  13.6         1.06     93.3 1472.    0.00151
## 10 Australia          1 2013-01-16  13.4         1.06     94.2 1473.    0.00151
## # ℹ 2,621 more rows
## # ℹ 20 more variables: VIX_lag1 <dbl>, VIX_lag2 <dbl>, VIX_lag3 <dbl>,
## #   VIX_lag4 <dbl>, VIX_lag5 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag1 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag2 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOil_lag3 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag4 <dbl>, CrudeOil_lag5 <dbl>,
## #   SP500_lag1 <dbl>, SP500_lag2 <dbl>, SP500_lag3 <dbl>, SP500_lag4 <dbl>,
## #   SP500_lag5 <dbl>, Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag1 <dbl>,
## #   Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag2 <dbl>, Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag3 <dbl>, …
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)
## 
## Attaching package: 'plm'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     between, lag, lead
# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}
## 
## --- Regression Summary for Lag 1 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", 
##     sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29

## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", 
##     sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:

for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 1 : 1.18757903053692"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 2 : 1.20210068527393"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 3 : 1.24488709656069"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 4 : 1.27340614938017"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 5 : 1.28390074204149"
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}
##

## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", 
##     sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max. 
## -0.00521549 -0.00178954 -0.00042069  0.00186299  0.00635951 
## 
## Coefficients:

## VIX_lag1      -1.9700e-03  8.7908e-04 -2.2409  0.034548 *

## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 1 : 1.1740055329479"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 2 : 1.18774525642708"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 3 : 1.23247070463044"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 4 : 1.26101141028973"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 5 : 1.27814394170624"
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

##     model = "within")

## CrudeOil_lag1  1.4607e-03  4.7705e-04  3.0620  0.005353 **

## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29

## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate  Std. Error t-value  Pr(>|t|)    
## VIX_lag3      -1.1937e-03  1.6395e-03 -0.7280 0.4736277    
## SP500_lag3    -4.3554e-04  9.8882e-05 -4.4046 0.0001888 ***
## CrudeOil_lag3  8.6668e-04  8.8117e-04  0.9836 0.3351474    
## RelDiffGDP     2.4231e+00  1.7317e+00  1.3992 0.1745413    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    0.0030796
## Residual Sum of Squares: 0.00038515
## R-Squared:      0.87493
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.85409
## F-statistic: 41.9746 on 4 and 24 DF, p-value: 1.684e-10
## 
## --- Regression Summary for Lag 4 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", 
##     sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max. 
## -0.00559992 -0.00280052 -0.00034283  0.00306458  0.00968728 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate  Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)  
## VIX_lag4      -0.00053943  0.00247340 -0.2181  0.82920  
## SP500_lag4    -0.00029786  0.00014876 -2.0023  0.05668 .
## CrudeOil_lag4 -0.00141091  0.00112031 -1.2594  0.22000  
## RelDiffGDP     1.72845670  2.31963918  0.7451  0.46342  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    0.0030796
## Residual Sum of Squares: 0.00040859
## R-Squared:      0.86732
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.84521
## F-statistic: 39.2225 on 4 and 24 DF, p-value: 3.3944e-10
## 
## --- Regression Summary for Lag 5 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", 
##     sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max. 
## -0.00794018 -0.00235295  0.00052977  0.00282789  0.00582549 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate  Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)   
## VIX_lag5      -0.00553580  0.00220697 -2.5083 0.019293 * 
## SP500_lag5    -0.00053851  0.00014956 -3.6005 0.001436 **
## CrudeOil_lag5 -0.00142287  0.00107867 -1.3191 0.199585   
## RelDiffGDP    -3.13054866  2.40472238 -1.3018 0.205331   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    0.0030796
## Residual Sum of Squares: 0.00035011
## R-Squared:      0.88631
## Adj. R-Squared: 0.86737
## F-statistic: 46.7767 on 4 and 24 DF, p-value: 5.4238e-11
# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 1 : 3.16045650991473"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 2 : 3.59495369215841"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 3 : 3.3493049154734"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 4 : 3.1309246338965"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 5 : 2.97678103142419"
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffCPI ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffCPI) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

## -0.0070874 -0.0039239  0.0001491  0.0038372  0.0107335

##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)

## # A tibble: 2,748 × 28
##    Country Country_ID Date           VIXPC     ERPC CrudeOilPC    SP500PC
##    <chr>        <dbl> <date>         <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 Japan            4 2013-01-02 -0.102    NA         0.00703   0.0125   
##  2 Japan            4 2013-01-03 -0.00817   0.00207  -0.00215  -0.00209  
##  3 Japan            4 2013-01-04 -0.0501   -0.0138    0.00183   0.00487  
##  4 Japan            4 2013-01-07 -0.000966  0.00525   0.000358 -0.00104  
##  5 Japan            4 2013-01-08 -0.0123    0.00424  -0.000429 -0.00324  
##  6 Japan            4 2013-01-09  0.0140   -0.00671  -0.000537  0.00266  
##  7 Japan            4 2013-01-10 -0.0232   -0.00295   0.00773   0.00760  
##  8 Japan            4 2013-01-11 -0.00964  -0.0119   -0.00277  -0.0000476
##  9 Japan            4 2013-01-14  0.00396  -0.00134   0.00206  -0.000310 
## 10 Japan            4 2013-01-15  0.00222   0.00778  -0.00914   0.00113  
## # ℹ 2,738 more rows
## # ℹ 21 more variables: RelDiffCPI <dbl>, VIXPC_lag1 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag2 <dbl>,
## #   VIXPC_lag3 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag4 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag5 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag1 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag2 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag3 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag4 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag5 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag1 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag2 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag3 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag4 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag5 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag1 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag2 <dbl>, …
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffUnemp ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffUnemp) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag2", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_2", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

## 
## Residuals:

## -0.0078026 -0.0027088  0.0002595  0.0031201  0.0067092 
## 
## Coefficients:

## # A tibble: 2,748 × 28
##    Country Country_ID Date           VIXPC     ERPC CrudeOilPC    SP500PC
##    <chr>        <dbl> <date>         <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 Japan            4 2013-01-02 -0.102    NA         0.00703   0.0125   
##  2 Japan            4 2013-01-03 -0.00817   0.00207  -0.00215  -0.00209  
##  3 Japan            4 2013-01-04 -0.0501   -0.0138    0.00183   0.00487  
##  4 Japan            4 2013-01-07 -0.000966  0.00525   0.000358 -0.00104  
##  5 Japan            4 2013-01-08 -0.0123    0.00424  -0.000429 -0.00324  
##  6 Japan            4 2013-01-09  0.0140   -0.00671  -0.000537  0.00266  
##  7 Japan            4 2013-01-10 -0.0232   -0.00295   0.00773   0.00760  
##  8 Japan            4 2013-01-11 -0.00964  -0.0119   -0.00277  -0.0000476
##  9 Japan            4 2013-01-14  0.00396  -0.00134   0.00206  -0.000310 
## 10 Japan            4 2013-01-15  0.00222   0.00778  -0.00914   0.00113  
## # ℹ 2,738 more rows
## # ℹ 21 more variables: RelDiffUnemp <dbl>, VIXPC_lag1 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag2 <dbl>,
## #   VIXPC_lag3 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag4 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag5 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag1 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag2 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag3 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag4 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag5 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag1 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag2 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag3 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag4 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag5 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag1 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag2 <dbl>, …
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffGDP ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffGDP) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag3", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_3", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

## # A tibble: 2,748 × 28
##    Country Country_ID Date           VIXPC     ERPC CrudeOilPC    SP500PC
##    <chr>        <dbl> <date>         <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 Japan            4 2013-01-02 -0.102    NA         0.00703   0.0125   
##  2 Japan            4 2013-01-03 -0.00817   0.00207  -0.00215  -0.00209  
##  3 Japan            4 2013-01-04 -0.0501   -0.0138    0.00183   0.00487  
##  4 Japan            4 2013-01-07 -0.000966  0.00525   0.000358 -0.00104  
##  5 Japan            4 2013-01-08 -0.0123    0.00424  -0.000429 -0.00324  
##  6 Japan            4 2013-01-09  0.0140   -0.00671  -0.000537  0.00266  
##  7 Japan            4 2013-01-10 -0.0232   -0.00295   0.00773   0.00760  
##  8 Japan            4 2013-01-11 -0.00964  -0.0119   -0.00277  -0.0000476
##  9 Japan            4 2013-01-14  0.00396  -0.00134   0.00206  -0.000310 
## 10 Japan            4 2013-01-15  0.00222   0.00778  -0.00914   0.00113  
## # ℹ 2,738 more rows
## # ℹ 21 more variables: RelDiffGDP <dbl>, VIXPC_lag1 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag2 <dbl>,
## #   VIXPC_lag3 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag4 <dbl>, VIXPC_lag5 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag1 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag2 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag3 <dbl>,
## #   CrudeOilPC_lag4 <dbl>, CrudeOilPC_lag5 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag1 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag2 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag3 <dbl>, SP500PC_lag4 <dbl>,
## #   SP500PC_lag5 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag1 <dbl>, Predicted_ERPC_Lag2 <dbl>, …
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}
## 
## --- Regression Summary for Lag 1 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, 
##     "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max. 
## -0.00715119 -0.00255461 -0.00051712  0.00244447  0.00753853 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)
## VIXPC_lag1      -0.0265768  0.0256871 -1.0346   0.3112
## SP500PC_lag1    -0.4742036  0.2979333 -1.5916   0.1246
## CrudeOilPC_lag1  0.0299579  0.1224030  0.2447   0.8087
## RelDiffCPI      -0.0015590  0.0031795 -0.4903   0.6284
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    0.00042078

## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 1 : 0.0309083333782947"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 2 : 0.0383356559631419"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 3 : 0.0368688344152455"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 4 : 0.0448019303446903"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 5 : 0.0291822610565744"
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

##     "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
##

##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max.

## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 4 : 0.0206584500578476"
## [1] "RMSE for Model with Lag 5 : 0.0209899980582919"
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, 
##     "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
##

##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max.

## VIXPC_lag1      -0.026577   0.025687 -1.0346   0.3112
## SP500PC_lag1    -0.474204   0.297933 -1.5916   0.1246
## CrudeOilPC_lag1  0.029958   0.122403  0.2447   0.8087
## RelDiffGDP       0.416957   0.850367  0.4903   0.6284
## 
## Total Sum of Squares:    0.00042078

## Adj. R-Squared: -0.037507
## F-statistic: 0.746942 on 4 and 24 DF, p-value: 0.56965
## 
## --- Regression Summary for Lag 2 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, 
##     "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##        Min.     1st Qu.      Median     3rd Qu.        Max. 
## -0.00587598 -0.00313325 -0.00076817  0.00260636  0.00820596 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)  
## VIXPC_lag2      0.0408021  0.0234499  1.7400  0.09467 .
## SP500PC_lag2    0.1876705  0.2794097  0.6717  0.50821  
## CrudeOilPC_lag2 0.0478669  0.1160207  0.4126  0.68358  
## RelDiffGDP      0.0087685  0.7985697  0.0110  0.99133

## --- Regression Summary for Lag 3 ---
## Oneway (individual) effect Within Model
## 
## Call:
## plm(formula = as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, 
##     "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")), data = train_window, 
##     model = "within")
## 
## Balanced Panel: n = 1, T = 29, N = 29
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min.    1st Qu.     Median    3rd Qu.       Max. 
## -0.0063837 -0.0025871 -0.0010989  0.0025168  0.0073886 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t-value Pr(>|t|)

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}
